items.title: Content prompt contract cleanup — natural structure and evidence-only specifics
items.notes: First dependency. Reconcile page-type outline/copy contracts with section prompts: do not force two H3s into every 200-word conversion section, and make experience counts, results, local landmarks/regulations/partnerships, social proof, product specs, guarantees, and similar facts conditional on approved supplied evidence. Preserve factual-safety, HTML, keyword, page-type density, and output-format contracts. Add deterministic prompt assertions for blog, service, location, landing, product, and healthcare fixtures. No model change.

items.title: One frozen content authority across draft, regenerate, AI fix, and repair paths
items.notes: After prompt cleanup. Make full brief regeneration, outline regeneration, section regeneration, feedback fixes, checklist fixes, and full-post revisions consume the same revision-fenced brief evidence, page-type contract, brand authority, and content-generation-context-v2 fingerprint as initial generation. Remove the invented /blog or /services internal-link fallback; without a verified target, return a needs-input result. Word-count expansion must use the brief/evidence and must not manufacture filler. Preserve review-before-apply, CAS, provenance, and legacy flag-OFF parity until v2 promotion.

items.title: Relevant approved brand, BrandScript, and E-E-A-T proof projection
items.notes: Primary owner brand-engine; content-pipeline consumes. Extend the approved-only brand projection beyond the six core fields with selected messaging pillars, differentiators, brand story, personas, and a compact approved BrandScript representation. Upgrade E-E-A-T prompt projection from title/type/URL labels to page-relevant approved excerpts plus attribution, source, evidence date, service/location tags, and page associations. Never inject drafts, raw authenticated review text, or the whole inventory. Tests must pin exact-once voice authority and relevance/cap behavior.

items.title: Content context v2 relevance selection — priorities, pipeline conflicts, copy learnings, and refresh evidence
items.notes: After approved-proof contracts. Add bounded, relevance-selected context rather than raising prompt budgets: resolved client/admin priorities; in-flight targets and cannibalization/decay conflicts; actual active copy-intelligence/rewrite patterns instead of a count; and targeted pageProfile/page evidence for refreshes. Protect the most relevant proof/local section from silent tail-dropping under the 800-token supplementary budget. Keep target-only page-map behavior, timestamped evidence, and staging p95/token gates.

items.title: Whole-document cohesion, voice, and evidence review with visible unification state
items.notes: Replace first-8,000-character-only review/voice coverage with bounded chunk review plus a final aggregate verdict. If the full-draft unification pass fails or is rejected, retain the valid draft but expose a needs-editorial-review state instead of treating cohesion as invisible non-critical loss. Measure repetition, structural variety, voice drift, unsupported claims, and page-type density without auto-clearing provenance-sensitive items.

items.title: Human-rated content golden set and operation-specific model canaries
items.notes: Final dependency after prompt/context/whole-document repairs. Build a small versioned golden set spanning blog, service, location, landing, product, and provenance-sensitive healthcare content. Human scoring covers naturalness, specificity, on-brand fidelity, structural variety, factual discipline, edit distance, cost, and latency. Then canary model candidates per named operation with rollback evidence; do not globally replace Claude Sonnet 4.6 or GPT-5.4 based on availability alone. Coordinate with genq-runtime-quality-governance.

Deterministic high-risk unsupported additions involving health, insurance, treatment, outcomes, finance/legal details, minors, or names block send/publish. Factual/provenance review always sees the complete current source.

Add cron-registry-owned read-only sync, paginate only far enough to cover the permitted operational window, consume continuation tokens, and reconcile edited/deleted/replied reviews. Purge expired source content and label operational backlog metrics as windowed. Keep Google-provided totalReviewCount/averageRating as profile-wide aggregates and test external failure truthfulness.

Compare GBP NAP/category/website against confirmed Business Footprint authority using confirm-before-overwrite. Measure draft/approval/publish time, success, and response coverage; durable reputation trends remain on public/DataForSEO snapshots.

items.notes: [BUILT + PR-READY 2026-06-20 on the-issue/phase-3-pushed-cron (HEAD 905556c97): 4 lanes + 2 adversarial review passes (P1a 7-dim + the capture-refactor 4-dim), all findings fixed incl. the unbounded-backfill date-floor; full suite 26704 green; flag-OFF byte-identical; D7 PII held. Not on staging — owner's promotion call.] P1a graduates the P0 GA4 ESTIMATE into a MEASURED, client-verifiable outcome behind a new flag the-issue-client-measured-capture (default OFF; OFF = P0 estimate-only spine byte-identical). Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-the-issue-client-p1a-plan.md. Adds a third provenance tier measured_action between estimate_ga4 (P0) and actual_reconciled (P3); event-type classification (OutcomeType form_fill/call/booking/email/directions/chat/other + IssueOutcomeCount.byType + EventDisplayConfig.outcomeType); a Webflow form-webhook capture substrate (form_submissions table mig 148 + HMAC-verified receiver POST /api/public/webflow-form-webhook/:workspaceId + workspace form-capture config mig 149) with idempotency; selectOutcomeProvenance graduating to measured_action ONLY on confirmed setup; computeROI().outcomeVerdict provenance seam + anonymous reconciliation count on the public payload (PII never leaks, D7). Feasibility verdict: GA4 = operator-guided consumption (no GTM/script-inject); Webflow form-lead = signed webhook the operator pastes (no programmatic registration). Forms-API polling + named-lead public click-through (P1b) + push/export (P1b/P1c) + CRM reconciliation (P3) deferred. Lanes: A (contracts + capture substrate + flag, dependency root) -> B (client typed/measured render) || C (admin setup flow) -> D (acceptance/contract/DOM-probe). Migrations 148/149. CAPTURE REFACTOR 2026-06-20 (owner directive): the HMAC webhook receiver was retired and replaced by Webflow Forms Data-API POLLING (the signed-webhook model needed an operator-pasted per-workspace secret that did not match reality). New: server/webflow-forms.ts (listWebflowForms + listWebflowFormSubmissions + mapWebflowSubmission/resolveOutcomeType), server/webflow-form-poller.ts (daily, FM-2 per-workspace, broadcasts FORM_SUBMISSION_CAPTURED + logs PII-free on new insert, flag-OFF no-op), registered in startup.ts. Admin router reworked to GET webflow-forms (picker) + PUT form-sources (save mappings + confirm setup), keeping GET status. webflowFormWebhookSecret column DROPPED (migration 150). Admin UI flips from paste-a-secret to select-forms-to-track. Storage/provenance/client render unchanged (source-agnostic). See FEATURE_AUDIT #530. Read-before-write: live Webflow v2 submissions endpoint is site-scoped (/sites/{site_id}/forms/{form_id}/submissions).

items.title: Client outcome reconciliation — CRM/HubSpot sync (down the road, after website-native capture)
items.source: Owner direction 2026-06-20 — defer CRM, prefer website-native capture first
items.notes: DEFERRED by owner (2026-06-20): clients are unlikely to want to sync CRM data right now. The P1 reconciliation path is REFRAMED to capture outcomes from the client's WEBSITE + analytics (assets the agency already accesses), NOT their internal systems: (1) GTM + GA4 event instrumentation (form submits, click-to-call tel:, booking/Calendly clicks, mailto, directions/map clicks, chat opens) marked as GA4 key events -> the existing eventConfig-pinned P0 consumer renders '23 form fills + 41 calls' (client-verifiable, no client system); (2) Webflow-native form capture via the existing Webflow integration -> real NAMED leads, no CRM; (3) GA4 enhanced measurement + custom dimensions; (4) optional later: call tracking w/ dynamic number insertion (WE-provision, not a client system). THIS item (HubSpot/CRM/Salesforce lead->opp->closed-won sync) is the FAR-DOWN-ROAD upgrade for clients who later want true revenue attribution from their own CRM. Do NOT build until website-native capture (the reframed P1) ships and a client actually asks for CRM sync. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: demand-triggered CRM/call-tracking upgrade and canonical owner of actual_reconciled. Re-enter only when a client requests closed-won revenue reconciliation and website-native capture is insufficient.

sprints.name: 🎯 Keyword Value — propagation, money, GSC signals, client display
sprints.rationale: Phase 0–2 of keyword-value-scoring shipped the value-first score into the Hub sort + content-gap spine, but a wiring audit found the new score is still siloed: it does NOT propagate to briefs / content-gen / titles-metas / schema / internal-linking / content-plan / AI-context, and the client briefing actively demotes it (sorts by raw volume). This sprint propagates ONE consistent value score everywhere, turns it into dollars, surfaces the per-keyword GSC data already collected-but-discarded (striking-distance, momentum), and builds the client-facing layer — all additive, reusing signals already in hand, no new data collection.
sprints.notes: Sequenced by effort-to-value with dependencies (item titles tagged Wave 1–5). Wave 1 makes the score honest+legible+consistent (foundation, unblocks the client display); Wave 2 = dollars; Wave 3 = surface discarded GSC signals (one per-keyword join → 3 surfaces); Wave 4 = the client keyword display layer; Wave 5 = opportunistic/deeper. Each item runs the established brainstorm→spec→plan→phased-PR flow and is flag-gated where it changes ranking/output. Audit source: 2026-06-05 conversation (10-agent wiring audit + diverse-lens ideation). || 2026-06-05 ANTI-DUPLICATION PLACEMENT AUDIT (6-surface inventory): each item annotated with its canonical HOME + verdict (exists / fold-into / new). DROPPED kwv-client-wins-this-month — the named rank-movement win already ships end-to-end (ranking-mover.ts -> signal-story-registry.ts:410 -> InsightsBriefingPage DataSpread); its only real need (value-rank which named win leads) merged into kwv-one-score-everywhere. ADDED kwv-titles-metas-schema-priority + kwv-internal-linking-value-priority to capture wiring-audit surfaces not yet tracked. Rule: enrich existing Insights/Wins/Recommendations/ROI/StrategyKeywordDrawer/PageKeywordMapContent surfaces — NO new client keyword tab, NO second dollar engine. items.title: API documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger spec)
items.source: Market prep analysis Mar 2026; revised May 2026 post-review
items.notes: 91 route files in server/routes/ with no formal API spec. Triggers to pull forward: (a) first external API consumer signed (paid agency white-label, Zapier, contractor onboarding), (b) committing to migrate response validation to Zod for other reasons (OpenAPI becomes a free byproduct), (c) hitting 'I don't know what this endpoint returns' friction during builds. Implementation: use @asteasolutions/zod-to-openapi — NOT tsoa (requires controller-class refactor incompatible with Express+middleware style) or swagger-jsdoc (hand-maintained doc blocks rot immediately). Prerequisites before start: (1) response schemas must be migrated from shared/types/ TS interfaces to Zod (currently only request schemas are Zod via validate() middleware) — this is the bulk of the work, not the spec emission, (2) per-route security tagging for the dual auth model (HMAC x-auth-token admin vs JWT client vs public token), (3) pr-check rule asserting every Express route has a registered OpenAPI entry to prevent drift (zod-to-openapi does NOT introspect the route table — registration is opt-in per route). Out of scope: WebSocket broadcasts (would need separate AsyncAPI spec). Deliverables: registered request+response schemas per route, /api/docs Swagger UI gated to authenticated admins only, generated spec in CI artifact, drift-detection test. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but correctly framed as a TRIGGERED/gated item, not an active backlog item. The note's gating triggers (first external API consumer, Zod-response-migration done for other reasons, or build-time 'what does this return' friction) are all unrealized for a solo-founder platform with no external API consumers. Do not pull forward until a trigger fires. The implementation guidance (zod-to-openapi, dual-auth security tagging, drift-detection pr-check rule) is still architecturally sound for the current Express+middleware style. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: MCP is the preferred guarded external action surface. Re-enter OpenAPI work only for a signed REST consumer or a deliberate response-schema migration.

items.title: Per-market keyword relevance — keyword↔market assignment model
items.notes: Today the refresh planner pairs every selected keyword with every active market — `dentist at houston` gets queried against Austin's market and surfaces Houston competitors as 'top local results' in the Austin context. Confirmed on Swish staging refresh 2026-05-21 (55 of 200 snapshots = 27.5% cross-market noise). Fix is non-trivial because it requires a keyword↔market assignment model. (1) New `keyword_markets` join table mapping which keywords should be checked against which markets. (2) Default: keyword applies to all active markets (matches current implicit behavior, no migration of existing data needed). (3) Override path: admin pins a keyword to specific market(s). (4) Auto-inference: if keyword text contains an active market's city/region name, default assignment to that market only. (5) Iterator + refresh planner respect the assignment. (6) UI: per-market detail blocks in keyword drawer instead of single block, with pin/unpin controls. (7) **Per-market volume/clicks/impressions reporting**: the keyword detail surface should show separate volume, GSC clicks, GSC impressions, and CTR per active market the keyword applies to — today these are workspace-global and don't distinguish between Austin and Houston performance for the same keyword. Requires per-market metric storage AND geo-targeted DataForSEO volume queries (see intel-quality-keyword-volume-geo-targeting). Deferred to P2 because no current client uses multi-market in production — Swish is the only multi-market workspace and is internal. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-confirm priority. The item is still framed correctly for the current architecture (the planner pairing it describes is verbatim what exists). The deferral rationale also still holds: per the notes, Swish is the only multi-market workspace and it's internal — so the 27.5% cross-market noise has zero production client impact today. Recommend keeping P2/deferred and gating activation on the FIRST paying multi-market client rather than building speculatively. When built, sub-points 1-5 (join table, default-all, pin override, city-name auto-inference, planner respect) are the load-bearing core; sub-points 6-7 (per-market UI + per-market metrics) depend on the also-pending geo-targeted volume item and should be a follow-up phase. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: candidate market tagging ships, but the assignment model should wait for a paying multi-market client and demonstrated cross-product noise.

Add coordinate-point/geographic grid tracking for local map visibility with strict caps, scheduling, cache/telemetry, and UI designed for local businesses. Use DataForSEO Maps/SERP capabilities or another provider only after modeling cost by keyword x coordinate x device x cadence. Keep geo-grid separate from GSC Rank Tracker snapshots and avoid auto-inferred grids from keywords alone. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant; correctly framed. This is the most expensive and most clearly deferred of the local items (8-14h + per-coordinate provider cost). The item explicitly states it should follow local-pack v1 AND cost validation, and remain separate from GSC Rank Tracker snapshots — all still accurate. Recommend keeping P2/deferred and treating it as a premium/add-on capability that only activates once there is a paying local-business client whose ARPU justifies the keyword×coordinate×device×cadence provider spend. Lowest urgency of the four intel-quality local items. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: coordinate-grid tracking remains a paid-provider cost multiplier. Re-enter only for a paying local client whose ARPU supports the recurring grid.

items.title: MCP content edit orchestration (server-side iterative sessions)
items.notes: Deferred follow-up after MCP read/write iterative editing v1. Scope may include edit-session lifecycle, staged deltas, and optional server AI wrapper endpoints while preserving existing service-layer writes, activity provenance (`source: mcp-chat`), and broadcast invalidation contracts. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · OWNER DECISION · high conf]: Escalate to owner. This is a deliberate conditional defer, not a missing capability. The v1 conflict/version/provenance safety net already covers the named risks, so there is no code-determinable trigger to build it. Keep the item parked as 'deferred — revisit only on observed operational pain'; do not promote to a build queue without evidence of high MCP edit conflict rates or failed-apply complaints. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OWNER DECISION / default close: current handle/revision safety remains sufficient. Re-enter only if telemetry or support incidents show repeated edit conflicts requiring durable server sessions.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Schema Intelligence & Authority
sprints.rationale: Highest strategic SEO differentiation and trust-evidence value. Prioritizes schema intelligence upgrades that improve rich-result eligibility, E-E-A-T signaling, and entity grounding.

items.title: Demand-gated Course list / physical Event schema
items.notes: Tier-5 monetization opportunity for agencies with productized services / paid content. Service.offers with Offer { price, priceCurrency, availability } — Google's 'service prices' SERP feature. Product schema for templates/digital products. Course for educational content/training. Event for webinars/launches with eventStatus + eventAttendanceMode (Online/Offline/Mixed). Each is independent rich-result eligibility unlock. Workspace.contentPricing already exists (used for content-purchase tiers); Service.offers can reuse the pricing infra. Per-type effort similar to parity-fields PR1's per-template additions; parallelizable across template files. Default-off; admin opts in per-workspace. Most useful for agencies with packaged services (web-design + brand audit + content retainer); skip for service-only sites without published prices. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, re-scoped to just Course + Event (+ optional PriceSpecification on offers). Drop Service.offers and Product from the title — both ship today. Course and Event are genuinely unbuilt and remain valid independent rich-result unlocks, but they're P2 and only matter for agencies with training/webinar content; the deliberate WebPage fallback (not an accidental gap) means this is a real net-new template build, parallelizable per the original note. Confirm demand before building — this may be needs-owner-decision on whether any current clients have Course/Event pages. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OWNER DECISION: Product and Service offers ship. Re-enter only for a qualifying client with a real course catalog or public bookable physical events; drop virtual webinar scope.

items.title: Emit supported Organization numberOfEmployees
items.notes: Tier-6 reputation graph fields. New Workspace.organizationAwards (string[] of Award names → Award entity[]), Workspace.organizationMemberships (industry orgs → ProgramMembership/Organization references), Workspace.organizationCredentials (certifications → EducationalOccupationalCredential), Workspace.numberOfEmployees (QuantitativeValue { minValue, maxValue, unitText: 'employee' }). Each emitted on Organization sibling node in homepage + local-business templates. Pure admin Settings UI + template extension; no AI/intelligence dependencies. Agency credibility in SERP, especially for B2B competitive queries. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P2. This is pure admin-Settings-UI + template-extension work with no AI/intelligence dependency (as the note says). numberOfEmployees is a near-free quick-win since the data field already exists — just add a QuantitativeValue emission to homepage.ts/local-business.ts. award/member/credential need 3 new Workspace string[] fields + Settings UI. Frame correctly as 'low-effort additive Organization fields'; the original 2-3d estimate is realistic given numberOfEmployees is half-done at the data layer. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] RESCOPE: retain only the small supported numberOfEmployees emission. Drop award/member/credential unless a named non-Google consumer creates value.

items.title: Schema Pillar 3 — Compile-time + CI Gates
items.notes: DEFERRED. Pillar 3 is regression-prevention hardening (schema-dts compile-time types + schemarama CI corpus + pr-check rule for cross-ref helper imports). It does not change schema output — it locks the bar Pillars 1 + 2 set. Decision (2026-04-29): defer until after schema-yoast-parity-fields ships, because the field expansion will reshape templates and Pillar 3's typed signatures would have to be rewritten. Reactivate after intelligence layer settles. Plan file remains committed for reference.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Team, Permissions, and Collaboration Controls
sprints.rationale: Unlocks multi-user adoption and safer account expansion by closing core team-management, role-gating, and collaboration workflow gaps.

items.title: Internal team management
items.notes:
Invite team members, manage roles, assign workspaces.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Internal-user backend (roles, invite, workspace assignment) fully shipped via /api/users; admin frontend UI to invite/manage internal team members is not built. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-scope to FRONTEND-ONLY: 'Build admin UI to invite/manage internal team members (list, invite, assign workspaces, set owner/admin/member role, deactivate)' wired to the existing /api/users endpoints. Backend is done. Estimate is now ~2-3h (UI + React Query hook + a new admin 'Team' page or Settings tab), not 4-5h. Low risk, P2 still fair given solo-founder (you are currently the only internal user). || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] KEEP: backend user/role CRUD ships, but no admin Team UI consumes /api/users. Remaining scope is inviting, editing, and deactivating internal users.

items.title: Permission-based feature access
items.notes:
Fine-grained: client_member view-only on approvals, member can't delete workspaces.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Role enum (owner/admin/member, client_owner/client_member) and requireRole middleware shipped; per-action gating (client_member view-only on approvals, member-can't-delete-workspace) is not implemented in routes.
[Platform health recommendation pass 2026-05-26: promoted due to partial migration risk (roles exist, per-action route enforcement still incomplete).] || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant. Re-scope to 'per-action authorization enforcement': add a client-role check in the client-portal auth path so client_member is view-only on approval/decision mutations, and (if internal members ever exist) gate workspace-destructive routes on internal role. Note the architectural subtlety in the re-scope: admin routes use HMAC not JWT, so 'member can't delete workspaces' only matters once internal members log in via JWT — verify that flow exists first. P1 is defensible as a security-hardening item, but real exposure today is low (you control all seats). || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] KEEP: client_member is not yet enforced as read-only at client mutation boundaries. Retain as the security/authorization closeout item.

items.title: Writer assignment
items.notes:
Assign content briefs to specific writers with notifications.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Adjacent infrastructure exists on work_orders (assigned_to column + mapper); content briefs lack any writer-assignment field and no notification flow is wired. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant. Re-scope precisely: 'Add a writer-assignment field to content briefs (or reuse work_orders.assigned_to), a UI to set it, and an email/in-app notification to the assignee.' Note this only pays off if you have/plan to have external writers — for a solo founder with AI-generated briefs it is low value today, so P2 is generous; consider needs-owner-decision on whether a human-writer workflow is even on the roadmap. If yes, ~2-3h is realistic since work_orders already has the column + mapper to model from. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: work orders support assignment, but briefs do not. Re-enter only when human-writer volume creates real assignment pressure.

items.title: Notification preferences
items.notes:
Per-user email settings, digest frequency.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] In-app notification feed shipped (NotificationBell + useNotifications); per-user email/digest frequency preferences are not built. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant. Re-scope to 'per-user email notification preferences (opt-out per event-type + digest frequency)', building on the existing CLIENT_NOTIFICATION_RECIPIENT_POLICIES table in notification-recipients.ts (the codebase already anticipates this as the branch point). Add a per-user prefs column/table and a settings UI. P3 is fair — low value until you have multiple seats per workspace generating notification noise. ~2-3h estimate is reasonable given the foundation. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: per-user notification controls wait until multiple recipients per workspace create real routing conflicts.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — UX Adoption & Onboarding Momentum
sprints.rationale: Improves activation and stickiness through customizable workspace surfaces, guided education, accessibility UX uplift, and onboarding automation.

items.title: Client Education Hub — Embedded SEO education throughout
items.notes:
Contextual tooltips with 'Why this matters'. 'Learn more' links to SeoGlossary. Video explanations for complex features. Education tip cards in empty states.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] SeoGlossary.tsx + SeoEducationTip.tsx (tab-keyed learnMore content for overview/performance/health/strategy/content/roi) ship; "Why this matters" framing, video embeds, and Glossary cross-links from tips are not wired. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, low priority (P3). Re-scope to the unbuilt delta: (1) reframe tip copy as 'Why this matters', (2) add Glossary cross-links from tips, (3) optionally empty-state education cards. DROP the 'video explanations' sub-scope unless the founder actually wants to produce video — heaviest, lowest-ROI part. Solid client-trust value but secondary to the diagnostics/attention items in this batch. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: inline education, glossary, and explainers ship. Revisit a standalone hub only if support data shows demand; omit the speculative video library.

items.title: Performance Budget Dashboard — Visual page weight tracker
items.notes:
Show page weight over time per workspace. Alert on regressions. Recommendations: 'Homepage grew 400KB, consider image optimization'.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] PageWeight + PageSpeedPanel + server/performance-store ship point-in-time data; historical weight trend per workspace + regression alerts are not built. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant for the unbuilt scope (historical trend + regression alerting), framed as partially-done. Re-scope/flag a prerequisite the note misses: the storage layer must change first — the current upsert-by-(sub,site_id) destroys history, so building a trend requires either keying snapshots by timestamp or adding a separate weight-history table. Low priority (P3) absent a client asking for weight trends. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: rebuilt Performance covers current state, while storage is not historical. Re-enter only for requested longitudinal performance reporting.

items.title: Workspace onboarding automation — templates, batch setup, reduce repetitive per-workspace tasks
items.notes: Each new workspace requires: connect Webflow, connect GSC, connect GA4, run keyword analysis, run site audit, set up brand voice, configure competitors, set content pricing, enable client portal. Most steps are identical across workspaces. Add: workspace templates (clone config from existing workspace), batch analysis trigger (run audit + keyword analysis + decay analysis in one click), onboarding checklist with progress tracking, default competitor auto-discovery. Separate initiative from intelligence engine — scoped as its own spec. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, re-scoped to the unbuilt remainder: clone-from-existing-workspace config and a one-click batch-analysis trigger are the two high-leverage pieces for a growing client base (the repetitive-setup pain the item targets). Drop the onboarding-checklist sub-bullet from scope (done). Re-frame the title to 'Workspace cloning + batch setup trigger' to reflect that the checklist half already shipped. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: checklist onboarding, competitor discovery, workspace creation, and start-job actions ship. Clone/template and batch convenience wait for onboarding volume evidence. || [PROGRAM RECONCILIATION 2026-07-13] Typed intake→brand→content orchestration is now explicitly owned by mcp-brand-deliverable-generation + mcp-brand-content-onboarding. Item 523 stays deferred and owns only workspace cloning/templates plus generic batch setup convenience.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Operational Automation & Quality Signals
sprints.rationale: Reduces manual operating overhead and increases signal quality with workflow automation, accessibility quality expansion, and strategy-memory follow-through.

items.title: Webhook / Zapier triggers
items.notes: Fire webhooks on key events. Enables automation integrations. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-scope and re-justify. The internal WS event taxonomy (server/ws-events.ts) is the natural source for outbound webhook fan-out, so the build is 'subscribe a user-supplied URL to existing workspace events' rather than greenfield event plumbing. Note for owner: the MCP action server may already cover the deeper 'enable automation' intent for AI-driven workflows, so this item is now narrowly about no-code (Zapier/Make) outbound triggers. Low priority (P3) is appropriate; validate demand before building. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: MCP owns agent automation. Re-enter outbound Zapier/Make webhooks only when a customer names a no-code workflow, scoped to durable event subscriptions.

items.title: Shared notes / wiki per workspace
items.notes: Team-internal notes about a client. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant and correctly framed, but DEDUPE against item 117 (which also lists 'shared notes') — fold the two shared-notes asks into one. Minimal viable build: a single workspace.internalNotes TEXT column + a notes panel in WorkspaceSettings (admin-only, never serialized to public-portal.ts). P3/2-3h is accurate. Genuinely useful even for a solo founder (durable per-client context/reminders), so this is a cheap, sensible keep — arguably the best ROI item in this theme. || [DEDUPE 2026-06-24]: owns the shared-notes capability that item 117's collaboration sub-scope also referenced — single source of truth. Minimal workspace.internalNotes TEXT + admin panel, never public-serialized. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] KEEP as the canonical small collaboration item: private per-workspace operator notes are still absent. Keep scope to shared notes with history, separate from AI knowledge context.

items.title: Client NPS survey
items.notes: Periodic in-dashboard satisfaction survey. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep — still-relevant, valid, and small. Re-scope to the current architecture: build it as a periodic in-dashboard survey component using existing primitives (SectionCard + a rating control), persist responses to a new typed table, and — importantly per the codebase's intelligence-slice rule — wire the resulting satisfaction signal into ClientSignalsSlice (server/intelligence/) so AdminChat/intelligence can see client sentiment (today it is blind to satisfaction). Low priority (P3) is appropriate given Premium/client base is still small; sequence it after there are enough active client users for NPS to be meaningful. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: re-enter at roughly 10–20 active clients or when churn analysis needs standardized satisfaction data and a real review cadence exists.

items.title: Historical redirect comparison
items.notes: Track redirect status over time, detect new 404s. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant; framing is correct. Note the storage is INSERT OR REPLACE on a timestamped id so prior scans are not reliably retained — a 'detect new 404s vs last scan' feature needs both (a) guaranteed retention of the previous snapshot and (b) a diff computation, neither of which exists. Small (2-3h) but genuinely unbuilt; reasonable to keep at P3 or fold into a broader 'site health over time' effort. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: current redirect scans/export ship but history/diff does not. Re-enter for requested new-404/regression alerts, preferably inside broader site-health-over-time work.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Integrations, Distribution, and Expansion Bets
sprints.rationale: Packages higher-latency integration and expansion work into a distinct growth track so near-term value sprints stay focused while long-horizon platform expansion remains actionable.

items.title: GSC: URL Inspection API
items.notes: Per-URL indexing status, crawl info. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant for the indexing/crawl half. Re-scope to 'extend the existing inspectUrl call to parse indexStatusResult (coverageState, crawledAs, lastCrawlTime, pageFetchState, indexingState) and surface per-URL index status', which is cheaper than the original estimate since auth + the API call + error handling already exist. Flag the dead inspectUrlForRichResults function to the owner as a separate cleanup question (built but unwired). || [CLEANUP 2026-06-24]: also surface inspectUrlForRichResults (search-console.ts) — built but has ZERO callers; either wire it into this feature or delete it. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: rich-results inspection exists but requested indexing fields remain unwired. Re-enter with a client indexing-diagnostic need and confirmed GSC quota posture.

items.title: Webflow Enterprise API: 301 redirects
items.notes: Push rules via API (Enterprise-only). || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but flag the Enterprise dependency for an owner decision: Webflow's redirect-rule WRITE API is Enterprise-only, so value is gated on whether any client is on Webflow Enterprise. Re-scope to 'add a write path that pushes scanner-recommended 301s to Webflow Enterprise redirect rules' built on top of the existing scan + recommendation output. Low priority (P4) is correct; build only when an Enterprise client materializes. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OWNER DECISION: CSV preparation/export ships. Direct redirect publishing waits for a confirmed Webflow Enterprise client with API access.

items.title: Sales report: email delivery
items.notes: Send reports directly to prospects. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant and genuinely cheap (the ~2h estimate is realistic) because server/email.ts:sendEmail + the existing HTML renderer already exist — this is just an endpoint + a prospect-email input in SalesReport.tsx. P4 is appropriate. Worth pairing with 89 if a lead-gen/outbound push is prioritized; otherwise leave parked. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: prospect reports ship, but email delivery has no proven outbound workflow. Re-enter when active sales cadence makes copy/send friction measurable.

items.title: Cross-client benchmarking network
items.notes: Anonymized benchmarks across all clients. Needs 20+ active clients. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · OWNER DECISION · medium conf]: Escalate to owner. This is a genuine founder judgment call on (a) whether a CLIENT-FACING cross-client benchmark is desirable/on-brand (vs keeping cross-client signal internal as it is today), and (b) whether the client base has reached the scale where it is meaningful and privacy-safe — the item's own '20+ active clients' gate is a business-scale decision, not a code state. If pursued, re-scope to BUILD ON platform_learnings_priors' anonymization contract (extend it to SEO-performance percentiles, not just action win rates) rather than starting fresh. Until the owner confirms scale + appetite, do not build. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OWNER DECISION: privacy-safe internal priors ship, but client-facing benchmarks require 20+ active clients plus explicit privacy and brand sign-off.

items.title: Backlink intelligence & outreach
items.notes: Ahrefs/Moz API → backlink profile → link gaps → AI outreach templates. Elevated from P3 — backlinks are a top-3 ranking factor and this capability justifies premium pricing. Lightweight overview (#213) ships first in Sprint F; this is the full-featured follow-up. 2026-06-04 groundwork: PR1 made DataForSEO the only runtime SEO/backlinks provider, removed SEMRush runtime registration, ungated DataForSEO backlinks, and converted strategy/competitive-intel/backlink UI copy to DataForSEO-first semantics. This item remains the follow-up for richer backlink intelligence (top backlinks, anchors, linked pages, outreach workflows). || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant for the unbuilt richer-intelligence + outreach scope; the roadmap's own 2026-06-04 note already correctly distinguishes this from the shipped overview. Re-scope to: (1) add provider methods getBacklinkAnchors / getTopBacklinks / getLinkedPages (DataForSEO MCP tools backlinks_anchors / backlinks_backlinks / backlinks_domain_pages exist), (2) competitor link-gap via backlinks_domain_intersection, (3) AI outreach template generation via callAI. P2 and premium-pricing justification still hold. Mark the prerequisite #213/overview portion as done. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] DEFER: DataForSEO backlink overview/referring domains ship. Re-enter anchors, link gaps, and outreach only when Premium demand justifies provider cost; Ahrefs/Moz framing is retired.

items.title: WordPress / Shopify support
items.notes: Abstract CMS integration layer. Only after Webflow PMF proven. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant; the item is framed correctly and its own gating note ('only after Webflow PMF proven') still holds. This is a large architectural undertaking (abstract the ~17 Webflow route modules + apply/publish paths behind a CMS-provider interface analogous to SeoDataProvider). P4 and PMF-gated are appropriate — do not start until a concrete WordPress/Shopify client demand exists. Founder decision on whether multi-CMS is the strategic direction. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OWNER DECISION: re-enter multi-CMS architecture only for a signed non-Webflow client and an explicit first-provider choice.

items.title: Embeddable SEO widgets
items.notes: Embeddable site health badge, traffic counter. Passive referral generation. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · OWNER DECISION · high conf]: Escalate to owner. This is a marketing/growth bet ('passive referral generation') whose value depends on go-to-market strategy, not a clear product gap. The technical lift is small (a public unauthenticated badge endpoint + embed script), but whether referral-driven distribution fits an agency model is a founder call. Keep parked at P4 pending that decision; if pursued, it pairs naturally with #119 marketplace distribution thinking. || [ROADMAP VALUE AUDIT 2026-07-12] OWNER DECISION: embeddable widgets are a distribution bet, not a platform gap. Re-enter only with an explicit referral strategy, ideally alongside marketplace distribution.

sprints.name: 🧭 Platform Simplification — Complexity Audit (2026-06-24)
sprints.hours: Mixed S–L; prioritize P1 structural fixes first
sprints.rationale: Findings from the 22-agent double-focused platform audit (2026-06-24). Captures complexity/simplification work NOT handled as immediate quick-win code fixes. Every item is evidence-backed with file:line refs. Full report: the audit synthesis (complexity lens).
sprints.notes: Sequence: P1 structural correctness/scale fixes first (activity_log retention, recommendation_sets normalize, facade cycles + madge ratchet, CTR-curve reconcile, normalizePageUrl disambiguate, verify:* triage), then P2 decompositions/tuning, then P3 sweeps. Multi-node readiness (P4) is a gated checklist — do not act until a 2nd instance is on the roadmap. || [DELTA AUDIT 2026-06-24]: F1 (snapshot retention + serp index, mig 157), F2 (auto-send activity logging), and the reserved-flag lifecycle marker were FIXED in the delta-audit follow-up PR; the items above are the un-fixed delta findings.

items.title: Outcome learning difficulty multiplier owner decision
items.notes: OWNER/SEMANTIC DECISION: outcome-learning-default-path.ts keeps DIFFICULTY_MULTIPLIER_ENABLED=false, so getDifficultyRangeLabel/difficultyMultiplier and the difficulty adjustment block are threaded but inert. Decide whether to fix the bin-unit mismatch and enable the multiplier with tests, or delete the difficulty path entirely. Do not bundle with behavior-free dead-code cleanup. || [TAIL TRIAGE 2026-06-27]: reclassified pending -> deferred because the next step is an owner semantic decision, not autonomous implementation. Re-entry: owner explicitly chooses enable-with-unit-fix or delete-the-path.

items.title: Multi-node readiness checklist (DO NOT ACT until a 2nd instance is needed)
items.notes: AUDIT high-confidence but CONDITIONAL — every item is correct on a single node TODAY; this is a future migration checklist, not a present defect. Gate behind "a 2nd instance is actually on the roadmap." Then: WS fan-out is in-process only (websocket.ts) — needs a shared bus; 16 schedulers start unconditionally on every node (startup.ts:20-39) → duplicate AI spend + emails — need leader election; background jobs run unbounded on the single event loop with no global concurrency cap and aren’t durable (restart marks in-flight "error") — need a durable queue; rate limiter + DataForSEO credit guard + SERP L2 file-cache are per-process — need shared state; single SQLite writer is the throughput ceiling — plan the Postgres/LiteFS path. Keep as the gating dependency doc behind any horizontal-scaling work. || [TAIL TRIAGE 2026-06-27]: reclassified pending -> deferred because the item explicitly says DO NOT ACT until horizontal scaling is actually needed. Re-entry: a second runtime instance or horizontal-scaling migration is scheduled.
